description_long: This bungalow features a steeply pitched, gabled roof with ornate wooden trim and dragon-head finials at the roof peaks, reminiscent of traditional Scandinavian stave architecture. The exterior is primarily constructed of dark wood, with a wide, wraparound porch supported by sturdy posts and railings. The roof is covered in light gray shingles, providing a striking contrast to the darker wooden walls. The front entrance is marked by a prominent, triangular portico with decorative cutouts and a small set of steps leading up to the porch. Multiple windows with pointed arches and grouped arrangements are visible on the upper and lower levels, adding to the building's historic and decorative character. The overall structure is broad and low, with a single main story and a high, open attic space under the roof.
description: A large, ornate wooden bungalow with a steep gabled roof, wraparound porch, and decorative trim, inspired by traditional Scandinavian stave architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a triangular portico, wide porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the gabled roof, grouped upper windows, and the back porch area.
description_view_2: Front view similar to view 0, emphasizing the entrance portico and decorative roof details.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to view 1, focusing on the roof's ornate trim and the arrangement of rear windows.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped upper windows
